entries.title: What is XS-Leak
entries.body:
Cross-Site Leaks (XS-Leaks) are vulnerabilities of the side-channel class: the attacker infers sensitive information about a target origin WITHOUT reading the response body.
The exploitation works not through data (as in XSS), but through observable browser behavior: differences in response time, side effects, error events.
They bypass classic CORS / SOP protections, because the attacker does not need to read the response - it is enough to distinguish two states (result present / no result, authorized / not).

entries.title: Attacker primitives (what leaks and through what)
XS-Search - a boolean oracle from search systems
entries.body:
XS-Search attacks abuse query-based search systems to extract a user's data.
By measuring a side effect of a search query (response time, frame count, error event), the attacker determines whether the search returned results or not.
This boolean oracle (yes/no) allows brute-forcing a secret character by character.

Example: open 50 tabs and use the timing difference from a CSP violation in an iframe on the search results page to brute-force the flag character by character.

entries.title: Principle of a timing attack
entries.body:
In a timing attack, information is extracted by measuring HOW LONG the system takes to respond to specific requests.
Scripts are delivered to the target application that hit the API, send AJAX, or initiate CORS interactions.
By measuring and comparing the response times of these operations, the attacker draws conclusions about internal behavior, data validation processes, or protective mechanisms.
Key point: the task is to distinguish two states by time, not to read the response.

entries.title: Frame Counting - principle
entries.body:
If a page loads a DIFFERENT number of iframes depending on the user's state (for example, by search results), the attacker counts them and infers data.
window.length on a window reference (window.open / iframe.contentWindow) returns the number of nested frames - this is precisely a boolean/numeric oracle available cross-origin (SOP does not block it).

entries.title: Frame Counting - PoC (window.length)
Cache Probing - principle
entries.body:
In a cache probing attack, a malicious site tries to determine whether a specific resource of the target site is already in the victim's browser cache.
The attacker forces the browser to request a resource (an image, script, endpoint) that could have gotten into the cache only if the user is authorized or previously visited a particular page.
By measuring how fast the resource loaded, or by observing the difference in behavior between a cached and a non-cached response, the attacker infers sensitive information.
A typical technique: flush the cache (with a CORS error or a POST request), then measure the reload.

rows: Detects resources loaded by a page. The cache is flushed with a CORS error.
Detects resources loaded by a page. The cache is flushed with a POST request.
Detects the Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header via popup.
Detects X-Content-Type-Options combined with a certain content type via CORB.
Detects the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header via fetch.
Leaks the target URL of a redirect via a CORS error.
Detects CSP directives via the csp attribute on an iframe.
Detects cross-origin redirects via a CSP violation event.
Leaks the target of a cross-origin redirect via a CSP violation event.
Detects the response body size by the length of the ETag header.
Detects errors via onload/onerror on an object.
Detects errors via onload/onerror on a script.
Detects errors via onload/onerror on a stylesheet.
Detects HTTP redirects via the Fetch API.
Detects the number of iframes on a page.
Detects javascript redirects via the History API.
Leaks the id attribute of focusable HTML elements via onblur.
Detects a server redirect by abusing the max redirect limit.
Leaks the dimensions of images or videos.
Leaks the duration of audio or video.
Detects status codes via the MediaError message.
Detects that another tab is using the Payment API.
Detects the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header via the Performance API.
Detects downloads (Content-Disposition) via the Performance API.
Detects empty responses via the Performance API.
Detects errors via the Performance API.
Detects X-Frame-Options via the Performance API.
Detects scripts/event handlers on a page via the Performance API.
Detects cross-origin HTTP redirects by the redirectStart timing.
Leaks content length via an SRI error.
Detects errors via a style reload bug.
Detects a server redirect by abusing the max URL length.
Detects the number of websockets on a page via socket limit exhaustion (Firefox).
Detects the number of websockets on a page via socket limit exhaustion (Chrome).

entries.title: Disk cache grooming
entries.body:
Disk cache grooming is a technique for manipulating the browser's disk cache to build an oracle.
Source (PoC): https://gist.github.com/parrot409/e3b546d3b76e9f9044d22456e4cc8622

entries.title: Tools for XS-Leaks
entries.body:
RUB-NDS/xsinator.com - XS-Leak Browser Test Suite (a ready-made set of oracle tests in the browser).
RUB-NDS/AutoLeak - finding XS-Leaks in the browser via diffing DOM graphs in two states.
